sectiondef.header: mipi_rx_phy Flags to Enable or Disable core

briefdescription.para: Watchdog timeout in HS mode Register.
Goto Stop state on timeout timer Register.
Clk lane PHY error Status Register.
Data lane 0 PHY error Status Register.
Data lane 1 PHY error Status Register.
Data lane 2 PHY error Status Register.
Data lane 3 PHY error Status Register.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XMIPI_RX_PHY_DYNAMIC_LINERATE_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the dynamic line rate.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XMIPI_RX_PHY_CTRL_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used for the enabling/disabling and resetting the MIPI_RX_PHY

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XMIPI_RX_PHY_INIT_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used for lane Initialization.
Recommended to use 1ms or longer in for TX mode and 200us-500us for RX mode

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and offset of XMIPI_RX_PHY_HSTIMEOUT_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used to program watchdog timer in high speed mode.

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and offset of XMIPI_RX_PHY_ESCTIMEOUT_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains Rx Data Lanes timeout for watchdog timer in escape mode.

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and offset of XMIPI_RX_PHY_CLSTATUS_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the clock lane status and state machine control.

detaileddescription.para: Only for RX

briefdescription.para: Bit offset for Control Error on Clock.
Bit offset for Stop State on Clock.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XMIPI_RX_PHY_DLxSTATUS_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the data lanes status

briefdescription.para: Set on Data Lane Esc timeout occurs.
Set on Data Lane HS timeout.
Set when MIPI_RX_PHY in ULPS mode.
Control Mode (Esc, Low, High) of Data Lane.
Bit offset for High Speed Abort.

sectiondef.header: Bitmask and offset of XMIPI_RX_PHY_HSSETTLE_REG_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used to program the HS SETTLE register.

sectiondef.header: K-Factor Values for Line Rate Ranges

briefdescription.para: CLKOUT0 Reg1: even divide, no edge.
CLKOUT0 Reg1: even divide, edge bit set.
CLKOUT0 Reg1: odd divide, no edge.
CLKOUT0 Reg1: odd divide, edge bit set.
DIV Reg2 HT/LT value for D=5.
Penalty for fractional M in score.

sectiondef.header: Math helper macros to avoid -lm dependency

briefdescription.para: Round double to nearest s32 integer.
Round double to nearest integer returned as double.
Floor of x returned as double.

sectiondef.header: PLL Register Offsets for Dynamic Line Rate Configuration

memberdef.name: XMipi_Rx_Phy_PllRegOffsets
memberdef.initializer: = { XPLL_REG_2_M_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_3_M_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_4_O1_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_5_O1_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_6_O0_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_7_O0_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_22_D_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_23_D_OFFSET, }

briefdescription.para: PLL register index to offset mapping (used in programming loop):

detaileddescription.para: PllVals order: REG_2_M, REG_3_M, REG_4_O1, REG_5_O1, REG_6_O0, REG_7_O0, REG_22_D, REG_23_D

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance provided by the caller based on the given Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
is the device configuration structure containing information about a specific Mipi_Rx_Phy instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: Configure the registers of the Mipi_Rx_Phy instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
to one of the registers to be configured
to be set for the particular Handle of the Mipi_Rx_Phy instance

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE If incorrect handle was passed

simplesect.para: There is a limit on the minimum and maximum values of the HS Timeout register.

briefdescription.para: Get if register interface is present from the config structure for specified Mipi_Rx_Phy instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: Get information stored in the Mipi_Rx_Phy instance based on the handle passed.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
to one of the registers to be configured

simplesect.para: The value stored in the corresponding register

briefdescription.para: This is used to do a soft reset of the Mipi_Rx_Phy IP instance.

detaileddescription.para: The reset takes approx 20 core clock cycles to become effective.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.

briefdescription.para: This is used to clear the Abort Error (Escape or High Speed) bits in the Data Lane 0 through 3.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
represents which Data Lane to act upon
contains information about which bits to reset

briefdescription.para: This is used to get information about Clock Lane status.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing which of the events have occurred along with the mode of the Clock Lane in Phy

briefdescription.para: This is used to get specific Lane mode information about Clock Lane.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing mode in which the Clock Lane in Mipi_Rx_Phy is in.

briefdescription.para: This is used to get information about a Data Lane status.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
for which the status is sought for.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing which of the events have occurred along with the mode of the Data Lane in Phy

briefdescription.para: This is used to get Data Lane Calibration status.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
for which the calib status is sought for.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS - Calibration Complete, Calibration packet received XST_NO_DATA - Calibration Complete, Calibration packet is not received XST_FAILURE - Calibration failed

briefdescription.para: This is used to get specific Lane mode information about a Data Lane.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
for which the mode info is requested.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing mode in which the Data Lane in Mipi_Rx_Phy is in.

briefdescription.para: This is used to get count of packets received on each lane.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
for which the mode info is requested.

simplesect.para: Bitmask containing mode in which the Data Lane in Mipi_Rx_Phy is in.

briefdescription.para: This is used to get Mipi_Rx_Phy Version.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.

simplesect.para: Returns major and minor Version number of this Mipi_Rx_Phy IP

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable or disable the Mipi_Rx_Phy core.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
denoting whether to enable or disable the Mipi_Rx_Phy core

briefdescription.para: This function configures the dynamic line rate for the MIPI RX PHY by programming the MMCM registers and NPI X5PLL registers.

detaileddescription.para: The driver disables the PHY, programs the MMCM via XMipi_Rx_Phy_MmcmConfig, delegates the PLL register programming to XMipi_Rx_Phy_XPllConfig, and re-enables the PHY.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
is the base address of the X5PLL register space.
is the desired line rate in Mbps (400-4500 Mbps).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if line rate configuration is successful.
XST_FAILURE if the line rate is out of supported range or dynamic line rate is not enabled in hardware.

simplesect.para: The PHY is disabled before MMCM and PLL reprogramming and re-enabled after the sequence completes to ensure a clean transition.

briefdescription.para: This inline function is used to set bit in a mipi_rx_phy register space.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit field to be set

briefdescription.para: This inline function is used to reset bit in a mipi_rx_phy register space.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit field to be reset

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift)
memberdef.name: XMipi_Rx_Phy_GetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the value of bitfield from mipi_rx_phy register space.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit field whose value needs to be obtained
is offset of bit field

simplesect.para: Bit Field Value in u32 format

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift, u32 Value)
memberdef.name: XMipi_Rx_Phy_SetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the value of bitfield from mipi_rx_phy register space.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP
is offset where the register is present
of bit field whose value needs to be updated
is offset of bit field
to be set. Passed in u32 format.

briefdescription.para: Look up the hardware configuration for a device instance.

parameterdescription.para: is the BaseAddress of the device to lookup for

simplesect.para: The reference to the configuration record in the configuration table (in xmipi_rx_phy_g.c) corresponding to the BaseAddr or if not found,a NULL pointer is returned.

briefdescription.para: This function programs the X5PLL registers for a given line rate.

detaileddescription.para: It looks up the matching bucket from the line rate table, then performs the PLL unlock -> configure -> re-lock sequence.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the X5PLL register space.
is the desired line rate in Mbps (400-4500 Mbps).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if PLL programming is successful.
XST_FAILURE if no matching line rate bucket is found.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the MMCM registers in the RX PHY for a given line rate.

detaileddescription.para: It computes the optimal MMCM parameters (M, D, O) and writes the corresponding values to the PHY MMCM registers.

parameterdescription.para: is the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance to operate on.
is the desired line rate in Mbps (400-4500 Mbps).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if MMCM programming is successful.
XST_FAILURE if no valid MMCM solution is found.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This test checks if HS Timeout value present in register matches the one from the generated file.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMipi_Rx_Phy instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the read value was not equal to _g.c file

briefdescription.para: This function reads a value from a MIPI_RX_PHY register space.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XCsiSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file).

briefdescription.para: This function writes a value to a MIPI_RX_PHY register space A 32 bit write is performed.

detaileddescription.para: If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XCsiSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file) to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

briefdescription.para: Derive the K factor from the line rate.

detaileddescription.para: The K factor maps line rate ranges to a divider used to compute the target output frequency: Fout = LineRate / K.

parameterdescription.para: is the desired line rate in Mbps.

simplesect.para: K factor, or 0 if line rate is out of range.

briefdescription.para: Pack high-time and low-time values into a 16-bit register value.

parameterdescription.para: is the high-time count (bits [15:8]).
is the low-time count (bits [7:0]).

briefdescription.para: Compute CLKOUT0 register values from the output divider.

parameterdescription.para: is the integer output divider.
is a pointer to store the first register value.
is a pointer to store the second register value.

simplesect.para: OutputDivider is assumed to be integer only.

briefdescription.para: Compute the MMCM parameters and DRP writes for a given line rate.

parameterdescription.para: is the desired line rate in Mbps.

simplesect.para: MmcmResult structure with Valid set if a solution was found.
Uses double-precision floating point arithmetic matching the reference MMCM C-code algorithm.

briefdescription.para: This function writes a value to a X5PLL register space.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the X5PLL instance.
is the register offset of the register to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Number of PLL registers programmed per bucket.
This file implements the functions to control and get info from the mipi_rx_phy.

detaileddescription.para: This file implements the X5PLL configuration for dynamic line rate support in the MIPI RX PHY driver.
Look up the hardware settings using device ID.
Contains diagnostic/self-test functions for the XMipi_Rx_Phy component.
This file implements the MMCM configuration for dynamic line rate support in the MIPI RX PHY driver.
Hardware definition file.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 pg 16/02/24 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: It defines the register interface.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 pg 16/02/24 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: It computes the optimal MMCM parameters (multiplier M, output divider O, input divider D) for a given line rate and programs the corresponding PHY registers. The PHY disable/enable is handled by the caller (XMipi_Rx_Phy_DynamicLineRateConfig in xmipi_rx_phy.c) so that both PLL and MMCM can be configured in the same disable window.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 ai 10/04/26 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: The hardware setting is inside the configuration table in xmipi_rx_phy_g.c, generated automatically by XPS or manually by the user.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 pg 16/02/24 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: It contains the PLL register look-up tables and the programming sequence used to reconfigure the PLL at runtime for a requested line rate.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 pg 16/02/24 Initial release
